title: Rich Creamy Irish Yellow Split Pea Stew
description: A hearty and comforting stew featuring fermented yellow split peas, perfect for a solo dinner, this dish is buddhist-temple-friendly and made with mushimono-steam method.
instructions: Rinse the fermented yellow split peas and drain well.
In a large pot, saute the chopped onion, minced garlic, chopped carrot, and chopped celery in unsalted butter until the vegetables are tender.
Add the vegetable broth, fermented yellow split peas, salt, and black pepper to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes or until the peas are very tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the stew until it is mostly smooth.
Stir in the heavy cream and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes or until the stew has thickened slightly.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
